title: Hemionitis horridula

The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hemionitis horridula include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusions, and is also used in the form of dried leaves or powder for the treatment of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Hemionitis horridula is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This classification places Hemionitis horridula within the division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a group of ferns characterized by their often leathery fronds and simple leaves.
